content_hints.description: The Yawning Portal's well drops into the first levels of Undermountain, where goblins, bugbears, and Xanathar Guild agents squabble over territory in halls that Halaster carved as an afterthought. These upper levels are almost civilized -- almost.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see the well shaft opens into corridors of rough-hewn stone where torchlight barely reaches the ceiling. The air carries the stink of goblin cookfires and unwashed fur, and somewhere ahead, raised voices argue in broken Common over territory that none of them built.
event_triggers: On entry: Levels 1-3. Recommended for parties of level 5-7.

content_hints.description: The Sargauth River cuts through these levels, its black water flowing from nowhere to nowhere. Drow outposts cling to the cavern walls, and the dwarven ruins of Clan Melairkyn give way to stranger architecture—temples to forgotten gods, prisons for things that should not be named.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see the river before you see it -- a deep, constant rush of black water that smells of iron and deep earth. The cavern walls bear the chisel-marks of dwarven masons, but the stonework changes as you descend, giving way to alien geometries and doorways carved in shapes that hurt to look at directly. Faint purple light pulses from somewhere ahead, and the air grows heavy with incense you cannot name.
event_triggers: On entry: Levels 3-5. Recommended for parties of level 7-9.

content_hints.description: The area of Narhnndellyn is clustered beneath the plateau of Qu'ellarz'url, allowing the superiors to look down upon them. This district remains the bright center of Menzoberranzan, where lesser houses vie for wealth, power, and influence amidst soaring, st각mitc towers and great manors.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a gray, sourceless light suffuse the cavern ahead, illuminating a landscape that has no right to exist this far underground. Stone trees stand in silent rows, their quartz bark glinting dully, their agate leaves frozen in an ancient wind.
event_triggers: On entry: Levels 6-9. Recommended for parties of level 9-13.

content_hints.description: The map reveals pathways to various locations, including the Castle Ward's sewers and the route through Undermountain and Skullport. It also points toward Xanathar's lair via a spiral staircase accessible from the sewers and hidden behind a secret door.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a spiral staircase accessible from the Castle Ward's sewers, hidden behind a secret door. The walls of this narrow, spiraling staircase are carved with opened eyes that glow with a faint, magical light.
event_triggers: On entry: Levels 10-16. Recommended for parties of level 13-17.

content_hints.description: The deepest level is Halaster's sanctum, where reality bends to the will of a thousand-year-old wizard who has had centuries to go mad and decades to get creative about it. Stars burn in underground skies, and doors open into rooms that should not exist. At the center of it all, Halaster Blackcloak waits, playing a game whose rules only he understands.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a vast void filled with swirling purple mist, and the air crackles with raw arcane energy that makes your teeth ache. Somewhere ahead, past corridors that bend in directions geometry forbids, a presence waits, patient and amused.
event_triggers: On entry: Levels 17-23. Recommended for parties of level 17-20.
